description_long: This is a portable metal crowd control barrier, commonly used in urban environments to manage pedestrian flow or restrict access to certain areas. The structure consists of a rectangular frame with rounded corners, supporting a series of evenly spaced vertical bars. The frame is supported by two wide, flat feet that provide stability on flat surfaces. On each side of the frame, there are hook-like connectors for linking multiple barriers together to form a continuous line. The entire object is constructed from tubular steel, giving it a robust and industrial appearance with a dark, matte finish. There are no visible reflective markings or additional features beyond the basic structural elements.
description: A sturdy, dark metal crowd control barrier with vertical bars and flat feet, featuring side hooks for interlocking with other barriers.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full rectangular frame, vertical bars, and both feet clearly, with side hooks visible.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the narrow profile of the barrier, highlighting the thickness of the frame and the flat foot.
description_view_2: Rear view nearly identical to the front, showing the vertical bars, frame, feet, and side hooks.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile and the flat foot, mirroring view 1.
